title: Topic, Headers, and Alternate Routing · Language Lens: Exchange Chain Cycle
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must parse topic segments, match bindings, model headers, and separate alternate custody from mandatory returns; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the exchange chain cycle increment in a deterministic Go routing and unroutable-decision table. Learners progress…
objectives: Use exchange-to-exchange bindings with bounded graph analysis, route evidence, permission checks, and cycle-safe topology review; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Route unmatched messages to an alternate exchange with observable ownership and distinguish it from mandatory returns; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Exchange Chain Cycle · Theory Exchange Chain Cycle
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 1153 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify primary, alternate, returned, and invalid routing outcomes; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then cancel the parent context while a result is still ambiguous. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use exchange-to-exchange bindings with bounded graph analysis, route evidence, permission checks, and cycle-safe topology review; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Exchange Chain Cycle contract for the supplied data.
